title: piptocarpha ramiflora
intro: Piptocarpha ramiflora, also known as "tree marigold," is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is a shrub that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Piptocarpha ramiflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and its extracts are also used in some pharmaceutical products.
